privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; no raw chat, health, pet-health, credentials, contact data, account identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.

memory_recovered.summary: A deterministic queue-item reconciler already existed but did not reject duplicate record identifiers, reversed input timestamps, status regressions, or conflicting projections recorded at the same instant.

durable_improvement.materialization_rule: materialization_allowed is false whenever any projection is rejected or a completion claim lacks required evidence. Source records remain immutable.

observed: The canonical queue remains stale relative to append-only projections.
The reconciler and expanded tests are committed.
inferred: Fail-closed materialization reduces the risk of silently converting contradictory owner projections into canonical state.
proposed: Add repository-wide discovery of queue projections and artifact-commit resolution before canonical materialization.
superseded: The prior reconciler behavior that accepted duplicate IDs and sorted away chronology concerns without explicit rejection.
unresolved: No authoritative process is yet named to materialize reconciled state into operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
No CI run was observed for the new commits.

falsification_or_review_route: Run the committed Node test in a fresh checkout, introduce each rejected condition independently, and reject this evidence if any invalid projection is accepted or materialization_allowed remains true.
